Work only in the current repo. Implement a minimal admin user-management page for the Residential Life portal.

Requirements:
- Add a frontend route/page for admin account governance.
- Use existing backend endpoints under /api/admin/users.
- Support: list/search users, change status to ACTIVE/DISABLED/FROZEN/BLACKLISTED, soft delete, show current status and purge timing if available.
- Restrict page to admin roles already used by the app.
- Keep UI simple and consistent with existing admin pages.
- Do not change backend contracts unless strictly necessary.
- If backend lacks a list/search endpoint, add the smallest production-ready endpoint needed.
- Add focused tests only if clearly valuable.

Return:
- What files changed
- Any assumptions
- What remains manual to verify

1	# Residential Life Operations Portal
2	
3	Local-only, on-prem portal for managing residential life operations: resident directory, housing agreements, room bookings, messaging, notifications, analytics, data collection, and local system integrations.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Quick start (5 minutes)
8	
9	### Prerequisites
10	
11	| Tool | Version |
12	|------|---------|
13	| Docker + Docker Compose | any recent |
14	| Java | 21 |
15	| Maven | 3.9+ |
16	| Node.js | 20+ |
17	
18	### 1 — Start PostgreSQL
19	
20	```bash
21	docker compose up -d
22	```
23	
24	By default the compose stack publishes PostgreSQL on port `55432` so it does not collide with a locally running database on `5432`.
25	Override it if you prefer:
26	
27	```bash
28	RESLIFE_DB_PORT=5432 docker compose up -d
29	```
30	
31	### 2 — Start the backend
32	
33	```bash
34	cd backend
35	./mvnw spring-boot:run
36	```
37	
38	Health check: `curl http://localhost:8080/api/health` → `{"status":"ok"}`
39	
40	Flyway runs all migrations automatically on startup. The dev seed data (V13, V14, V17) populates sample residents, agreements, messages, notifications, and the three login accounts below.
41	
42	### 3 — Start the frontend
43	
44	```bash
45	cd frontend
46	npm install
47	npm start
48	```
49	
50	Opens at http://localhost:3000. The `proxy` field in `package.json` forwards all `/api/**` calls to the backend on port 8080.
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Dev login accounts
55	
56	All passwords are `password` (BCrypt cost 12, matching the production encoder).
57	
58	| Username | Role | Email |
59	|----------|------|-------|
60	| `admin` | Admin | admin@reslife.local |
61	| `staff` | Residence Staff | staff@reslife.local |
62	| `student` | Student | student@reslife.local |
63	
64	> **Reseed note:** If you need to recreate dev passwords (e.g. after a schema reset),
65	> migration V17 re-hashes them at cost 12 automatically on next startup.
66	
67	---
68	
69	## Architecture overview
70	
71	```
72	browser (React 18)
73	    │  HTTP + cookie session
74	    ▼
75	Spring Boot 3.2 / Java 21   ← port 8080
76	    │  JDBC (Spring Data JPA + JdbcTemplate)
77	    ▼
78	PostgreSQL 16               ← port 55432
79	```
80	
81	### Backend layout
82	
83	```
84	backend/src/main/java/com/reslife/api/
85	├── common/          BaseEntity, SoftDeletableEntity, error handlers
86	├── config/          Security, CSRF, virtual-thread executor, crawler scheduler
87	├── admin/           Admin-only REST controllers (analytics, crawler, integration keys)
88	├── domain/
89	│   ├── auth/        Login, session, user management
90	│   ├── resident/    Resident directory + housing agreements
91	│   ├── messaging/   Message threads + notifications
92	│   ├── integration/ HMAC signing, rate limiter, webhooks, audit log
93	│   ├── crawler/     Multi-source data collection engine + scheduler
94	│   └── analytics/   Snapshot compute service (runs every 15 min)
95	└── ReslifeApplication.java
96	```
97	
98	### Database migrations (Flyway)
99	
100	| Version | Description |
101	|---------|-------------|
102	| V1 | Initial schema (users, roles, sessions) |
103	| V2 | Auth schema refinements |
104	| V3 | Data protection fields |
105	| V4 | Resident directory |
106	| V5 | Resident form fields |
107	| V6 | Agreement attachments |
108	| V7 | Messaging enhancements |
109	| V8 | Notification templates |
110	| V9 | Booking policy seed |
111	| V10 | Integration schema (keys, webhooks, audit log) |
112	| V11 | Crawler schema (sources, jobs, pages) |
113	| V12 | Analytics snapshots table |
114	| V13 | Dev seed: buildings, residents, agreements, move-ins, notifications |
115	| V14 | Dev seed: login accounts (admin/staff/student), messages |
116	| V15 | Audit log actor snapshot + ON DELETE SET NULL FK |
117	| V16 | Message delivery receipts (DELIVERED status) |
118	| V17 | Re-hash dev seed passwords at BCrypt cost 12 |
119	
120	---
121	
122	## Key features
123	
124	### Analytics dashboard (`/admin/analytics`)
125	
126	Four pre-computed metrics refreshed every 15 minutes (and on startup):
127	
128	- **Booking conversion** — housing agreement status breakdown + 6-month trend
129	- **No-show rate** — move-in check-in status breakdown + monthly no-show trend
130	- **Slot utilization** — occupied vs. total rooms, by building
131	- **Settlement completion** — acknowledged-notification completion by category
132	
133	Metrics are cached in `analytics_snapshots` and served without hitting aggregation queries on every page load. Use "Refresh now" to force an immediate recompute.
134	
135	### Data collector (`/admin/crawler`)
136	
137	Configurable multi-source crawl engine for intranet/local sites:
138	
139	- Per-source cron or fixed-interval schedule
140	- Resumable checkpoints (survives restart mid-crawl)
141	- Concurrency cap (default 5 parallel fetchers)
142	- Per-source throttle delay and max-depth/max-pages limits
143	- Pause, resume, and cancel individual jobs via the UI
144	
145	### Local integrations (`/admin/integration-keys`)
146	
147	HMAC-SHA256 signed requests for on-prem devices. See [INTEGRATION.md](INTEGRATION.md) for the full signing protocol.
148	
149	- Outgoing webhooks are restricted to private/local IP addresses (SSRF prevention)
150	- Rate limited: 60 requests per minute per integration key
151	- Replay protection: timestamps must be within 5 minutes of server clock
152	
153	---
154	
155	## Running tests
156	
157	### Backend (JUnit 5)
158	
159	```bash
160	cd backend
161	./mvnw test
162	```
163	
164	Unit tests cover the three stateless integration-layer services — `HmacService`, `IntegrationRateLimiter`, and `LocalNetworkValidator` — with no Spring context required (fast).
165	
166	### Frontend (React Testing Library)
167	
168	```bash
169	cd frontend
170	npm test
171	```
172	
173	Smoke tests verify that the app shell and login page render without errors.
174	
175	---
176	
177	## Offline constraints
178	
179	This system is designed to operate **without internet access**:
180	
181	- No CDN dependencies — all JS/CSS is bundled by `react-scripts`
182	- No external chart library — analytics uses pure SVG rendered in React
183	- No external fonts or icon sets
184	- Docker image `postgres:16-alpine` must be pulled once; thereafter fully offline
185	- Outgoing webhooks are hard-blocked from reaching public IPs by `LocalNetworkValidator`
186	
187	---
188	
189	## Database connection
190	
191	Default credentials (local dev only — change in production):
192	
193	| Field | Value |
194	|-------|-------|
195	| Host | localhost |
196	| Port | 55432 |
197	| Database | reslife |
198	| User | reslife |
199	| Password | reslife |
200	
201	Override via environment variables: `SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL`, `SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME`, `SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD`, or `RESLIFE_DB_PORT`.
202	
203	---
204	
205	## Known gaps and remaining work
206	
207	| Area | Status | Notes |
208	|------|--------|-------|
209	| Rooms & maintenance | Placeholder routes | UI not built; backend schema exists in early migrations |
210	| Incidents | Placeholder route | Not yet implemented |
211	| Resident form | Implemented | Guided edit flow exists; bulk-import CSV works |
212	| Booking policy enforcement | Partial | Rules are versioned and enforced by `POST /api/residents/{id}/booking-policy-check`, but there is still no full resident-facing booking workflow |
213	| Resident-facing booking UI | Not built | Staff can manage manually; no self-service room selection |
214	| Email / push notifications | Not implemented | Notifications are in-app only |
215	| Session store | JDBC-backed | Spring Session persists sessions in PostgreSQL |
216	| Production hardening | Not done | No TLS config, no rate limiting on auth endpoints, no audit log for admin actions |
217	| File uploads | Implemented for agreements | Staff can upload, list, download, and delete agreement attachments |
218	| Crawler content indexing | Raw HTML stored | No full-text search or NLP extraction layer |
219

2	 * Role constants and permission helpers.
3	 *
4	 * Roles match the backend RoleName enum (without the ROLE_ prefix used
5	 * internally by Spring Security).
6	 */
7	
8	export const ROLES = {
9	  ADMIN:                 'ADMIN',
10	  DIRECTOR:              'DIRECTOR',
11	  HOUSING_ADMINISTRATOR: 'HOUSING_ADMINISTRATOR',
12	  RESIDENT_DIRECTOR:     'RESIDENT_DIRECTOR',
13	  RESIDENT_ASSISTANT:    'RESIDENT_ASSISTANT',
14	  RESIDENCE_STAFF:       'RESIDENCE_STAFF',
15	  STAFF:                 'STAFF',
16	  STUDENT:               'STUDENT',
17	  READ_ONLY:             'READ_ONLY',
18	};
19	
20	export const ADMIN_PORTAL_ROLES = [
21	  ROLES.ADMIN,
22	  ROLES.HOUSING_ADMINISTRATOR,
23	];
24	
25	/**
26	 * Roles whose holders may see sensitive resident data (DOB, emergency contacts).
27	 * Must mirror SensitiveAccessLevel.FULL_ACCESS_ROLES on the backend.
28	 */
29	const SENSITIVE_ACCESS_ROLES = new Set([
30	  ROLES.ADMIN,
31	  ROLES.HOUSING_ADMINISTRATOR,
32	  ROLES.DIRECTOR,
33	  ROLES.RESIDENT_DIRECTOR,
34	  ROLES.RESIDENT_ASSISTANT,
35	  ROLES.RESIDENCE_STAFF,
36	  ROLES.STAFF,
37	]);
38	
39	/** Returns true if the user has the specified role. */
40	export function hasRole(user, role) {
41	  return Array.isArray(user?.roles) && user.roles.includes(role);
42	}
43	
44	/** Returns true if the user has at least one of the specified roles. */
45	export function hasAnyRole(user, roles) {
46	  return roles.some(r => hasRole(user, r));
47	}
48	
49	/**
50	 * Returns true if the user's role grants access to sensitive fields.
51	 * When true, the frontend shows a per-field "Reveal" button.
52	 * When false, the field is shown as "Restricted" (backend returned null).
53	 */
54	export function canRevealSensitive(user) {
55	  return Array.isArray(user?.roles) &&
56	    user.roles.some(r => SENSITIVE_ACCESS_ROLES.has(r));
57	}
58	
59	/** Returns true if the user can access admin configuration screens. */
60	export function canAccessAdmin(user) {
61	  return hasAnyRole(user, ADMIN_PORTAL_ROLES);
62	}
63	
64	/** Returns true if the user has the STUDENT role. */
65	export function isStudent(user) {
66	  return hasRole(user, ROLES.STUDENT);
67	}
68	
69	/**
70	 * Returns true if the user may browse the resident directory.
71	 * Mirrors STAFF_ROLES on the backend (ResidentController).
72	 * Students are excluded — they access their own record via /me.
73	 */
74	export function canViewResidentDirectory(user) {
75	  return hasAnyRole(user, [
76	    ROLES.ADMIN,
77	    ROLES.HOUSING_ADMINISTRATOR,
78	    ROLES.DIRECTOR,
79	    ROLES.RESIDENT_DIRECTOR,
80	    ROLES.RESIDENT_ASSISTANT,
81	    ROLES.RESIDENCE_STAFF,
82	    ROLES.STAFF,
83	  ]);
84	}

90	    [ROLES.DIRECTOR]:              'Director',
91	    [ROLES.HOUSING_ADMINISTRATOR]: 'Housing Administrator',
92	    [ROLES.RESIDENT_DIRECTOR]:     'Resident Director',
93	    [ROLES.RESIDENT_ASSISTANT]:    'Resident Assistant',
94	    [ROLES.RESIDENCE_STAFF]:       'Residence Staff',
95	    [ROLES.STAFF]:                 'Staff',
96	    [ROLES.STUDENT]:               'Student',
97	    [ROLES.READ_ONLY]:             'Read Only',
98	  };
99	  return labels[role] ?? role;

5	
6	-- Roles
7	CREATE TABLE roles (
8	    id           UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
9	    name         VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL UNIQUE,
10	    description  TEXT,
11	    created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
12	    updated_at   TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
13	);
14	
15	-- Users
16	CREATE TABLE users (
17	    id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
18	    username      VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
19	    email         VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
20	    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
21	    first_name    VARCHAR(100),
22	    last_name     VARCHAR(100),
23	    is_active     BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
24	    deleted_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
25	    created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
26	    updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
27	);
28	
29	-- User ↔ Role join (with timestamp)
30	CREATE TABLE user_roles (
31	    user_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
32	    role_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
33	    assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
34	    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, role_id)
35	);
36	
37	-- Residents / Students
38	CREATE TABLE residents (
39	    id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
40	    student_id        VARCHAR(50)  UNIQUE,
41	    first_name        VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
42	    last_name         VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
43	    email             VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
44	    phone             VARCHAR(30),
45	    date_of_birth     DATE,
46	    enrollment_status VARCHAR(50),
47	    room_number       VARCHAR(20),
48	    building_name     VARCHAR(100),
49	    deleted_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
50	    created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
51	    updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
52	);
53	
54	-- Emergency Contacts
55	CREATE TABLE emergency_contacts (
56	    id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
57	    resident_id UUID         NOT NULL REFERENCES residents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
58	    name        VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
59	    relationship VARCHAR(100),
60	    phone       VARCHAR(30)  NOT NULL,
61	    email       VARCHAR(255),
62	    is_primary  BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
63	    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
64	    updated_at  TIMESTAMPTZ  NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
65	);
66	
67	-- Move-In Records
68	CREATE TABLE move_in_records (
69	    id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
70	    resident_id     UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES residents(id),
71	    room_number     VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
72	    building_name   VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
73	    move_in_date    DATE        NOT NULL,
74	    move_out_date   DATE,
75	    check_in_status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
76	    recorded_by     UUID        REFERENCES users(id),

78	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
79	    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
80	);
81	
82	-- Housing Agreements
83	CREATE TABLE housing_agreements (
84	    id             UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
85	    resident_id    UUID         NOT NULL REFERENCES residents(id),
86	    agreement_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,

98	CREATE TABLE message_threads (
99	    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
100	    subject             VARCHAR(255),
101	    thread_type         VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DIRECT',

108	-- Thread Participants

115	
116	-- Messages
117	CREATE TABLE messages (

125	    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
126	);
127	
128	-- Notifications
129	CREATE TABLE notifications (
130	    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
131	    recipient_id        UUID         NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
132	    title               VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,

142	
143	-- Configuration Versions
144	CREATE TABLE configuration_versions (
145	    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

155	);
156

1	-- ============================================================
2	-- V2__auth_schema.sql
3	-- Authentication extensions:
4	--   • Replaces boolean is_active with account_status enum
5	--   • Adds scheduled_purge_at for 30-day soft-delete lifecycle
6	--   • Adds login_attempts for rolling-window lockout tracking
7	--   • Adds Spring Session JDBC tables (managed by Flyway, not
8	--     Spring Session auto-init, because ddl-auto=validate)
9	-- ============================================================
10	
11	-- ============================================================
12	-- 1. Extend the users table
13	-- ============================================================
14	
15	ALTER TABLE users
16	    DROP   COLUMN is_active,
17	    ADD    COLUMN account_status   VARCHAR(20)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
18	    ADD    COLUMN status_reason    TEXT,
19	    ADD    COLUMN status_changed_by UUID        REFERENCES users(id),
20	    ADD    COLUMN status_changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
21	    ADD    COLUMN scheduled_purge_at TIMESTAMPTZ;
22	
23	ALTER TABLE users
24	    ADD CONSTRAINT chk_users_account_status
25	    CHECK (account_status IN ('ACTIVE', 'DISABLED', 'FROZEN', 'BLACKLISTED'));
26	
27	CREATE INDEX idx_users_account_status
28	    ON users(account_status) WHERE account_status != 'ACTIVE';
29	
30	CREATE INDEX idx_users_scheduled_purge
31	    ON users(scheduled_purge_at) WHERE scheduled_purge_at IS NOT NULL;
32	
33	-- ============================================================
34	-- 2. Login attempt log (rolling-window lockout + audit trail)
35	-- ============================================================
36	
37	CREATE TABLE login_attempts (
38	    id           UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
39	    -- NULL user_id means the username was not found (unknown identifier).
40	    user_id      UUID        REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
41	    -- Raw identifier the caller supplied (username or email).
42	    identifier   VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
43	    ip_address   VARCHAR(45),
44	    user_agent   TEXT,
45	    succeeded    BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
46	    attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
47	);
48	
49	-- Fast lookup: count recent failures for a specific user
50	CREATE INDEX idx_login_attempts_user_window
51	    ON login_attempts(user_id, attempted_at DESC)
52	    WHERE succeeded = FALSE;
53	
54	-- Housekeeping: clean up old rows
55	CREATE INDEX idx_login_attempts_cleanup
56	    ON login_attempts(attempted_at);
57	
58	-- ============================================================
59	-- 3. Spring Session JDBC tables
60	--    Column types follow the official Spring Session schema for
61	--    PostgreSQL. Must match the version of spring-session-jdbc
62	--    pulled in by Spring Boot 3.2.x (spring-session-core 3.2.x).
63	-- ============================================================
64	
65	CREATE TABLE spring_session (
66	    primary_id            CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
67	    session_id            CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
68	    creation_time         BIGINT       NOT NULL,
69	    last_access_time      BIGINT       NOT NULL,
70	    max_inactive_interval INT          NOT NULL,
71	    expiry_time           BIGINT       NOT NULL,
72	    principal_name        VARCHAR(100),
73	    CONSTRAINT spring_session_pk PRIMARY KEY (primary_id)
74	);
75	
76	CREATE UNIQUE INDEX spring_session_ix1 ON spring_session (session_id);
77	CREATE INDEX        spring_session_ix2 ON spring_session (expiry_time);
78	CREATE INDEX        spring_session_ix3 ON spring_session (principal_name);
79	
80	CREATE TABLE spring_session_attributes (
81	    session_primary_id CHAR(36)     NOT NULL,
82	    attribute_name     VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
83	    attribute_bytes    BYTEA        NOT NULL,
84	    CONSTRAINT spring_session_attributes_pk PRIMARY KEY (session_primary_id, attribute_name),
85	    CONSTRAINT spring_session_attributes_fk FOREIGN KEY (session_primary_id)
86	        REFERENCES spring_session (primary_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
87	);

2	-- V15__audit_log_actor_snapshot.sql
3	--
4	-- Allows user rows to be hard-purged without violating the
5	-- audit_logs FK, while keeping actor identity readable.
6	--
7	-- Two changes:
8	--   1. Replace ON DELETE RESTRICT (default) with ON DELETE SET NULL
9	--      so that purging a user nulls the FK instead of blocking DELETE.
10	--   2. Add actor_username / actor_email snapshot columns that are
11	--      populated at write time; these survive the purge intact.
12	-- ============================================================
13	
14	-- 1. Swap the FK constraint to SET NULL
15	ALTER TABLE audit_logs
16	    DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS audit_logs_user_id_fkey;
17	
18	ALTER TABLE audit_logs
19	    ADD CONSTRAINT audit_logs_user_id_fkey
20	        FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
21	
22	-- 2. Actor snapshot columns (idempotent)
23	ALTER TABLE audit_logs
24	    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS actor_username VARCHAR(100),
25	    ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS actor_email    VARCHAR(255);

1	# ResLife Local Integration Guide
2	
3	This document describes how on-prem devices and systems authenticate with the ResLife
4	integration API and how to verify outgoing webhook signatures.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Overview
9	
10	All integration traffic is restricted to the **local network**.  Outgoing webhooks will
11	only be delivered to URLs that resolve to private IP addresses.  Inbound requests to
12	`/api/integrations/**` use HMAC-SHA256 signatures instead of session-based auth.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Integration keys
17	
18	An admin creates an integration key at **Admin → Integration Keys**.  At creation time
19	a plaintext secret is shown **once** — store it immediately.
20	
21	Each key has:
22	- **Key ID** — public identifier, sent in `X-Integration-Key-ID`
23	- **Secret** — shared HMAC secret used to sign requests
24	
25	---
26	
27	## Signing inbound requests (device → ResLife)
28	
29	When your device or service calls a ResLife integration endpoint it must include three
30	headers:
31	
32	| Header | Value |
33	|---|---|
34	| `X-Integration-Key-ID` | Your public key ID (e.g. `rk_abc123…`) |
35	| `X-Reslife-Timestamp` | Current Unix epoch **seconds** (integer) |
36	| `X-Reslife-Signature` | `sha256=` followed by the hex-encoded HMAC (see below) |
37	
38	### Signing algorithm
39	
40	```
41	signing_string = timestamp + "\n" + raw_request_body
42	signature      = HMAC-SHA256(secret, signing_string)
43	header_value   = "sha256=" + hex(signature)
44	```
45	
46	The server rejects requests where `|server_time − timestamp| > 300` seconds (5-minute
47	replay window).
48	
49	### Example — Python
50	
51	```python
52	import hashlib, hmac, time, requests
53	
54	KEY_ID = "rk_abc123..."
55	SECRET = "your-plaintext-secret"
56	
57	body      = '{"deviceId":"door-1","status":"unlocked"}'
58	timestamp = str(int(time.time()))
59	
60	signing_string = (timestamp + "\n" + body).encode()
61	signature = hmac.new(SECRET.encode(), signing_string, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
62	
63	requests.post(
64	    "http://reslife.local/api/integrations/events/door.unlocked",
65	    data=body,
66	    headers={
67	        "Content-Type":         "application/json",
68	        "X-Integration-Key-ID": KEY_ID,
69	        "X-Reslife-Timestamp":  timestamp,
70	        "X-Reslife-Signature":  "sha256=" + signature,
71	        "X-Request-ID":         "optional-idempotency-id",
72	    },
73	)
74	```
75	
76	### Example — curl
77	
78	```bash
79	TIMESTAMP=$(date +%s)
80	BODY='{"deviceId":"door-1","status":"unlocked"}'
81	SECRET="your-plaintext-secret"
82	
83	SIG=$(printf '%s\n%s' "$TIMESTAMP" "$BODY" \
84	      | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac "$SECRET" -hex \
85	      | awk '{print "sha256="$2}')
86	
87	curl -X POST http://reslife.local/api/integrations/events/door.unlocked \
88	  -H "Content-Type: application/json"         \
89	  -H "X-Integration-Key-ID: rk_abc123..."     \
90	  -H "X-Reslife-Timestamp: $TIMESTAMP"        \
91	  -H "X-Reslife-Signature: $SIG"              \
92	  -d "$BODY"
93	```

```java
@Transactional
public User updateAccountStatus(UUID targetUserId, AccountStatus newStatus, String reason,
                                UUID actorUserId, HttpServletRequest httpRequest) {
  // Guard: admin cannot change own status
  if (targetUserId.equals(actorUserId)) throw new IllegalArgumentException(...)
  
  // Load target user
  User target = userRepository.findById(targetUserId).orElseThrow(...)
  
  // Cannot change soft-deleted accounts
  if (target.isDeleted()) throw new IllegalArgumentException(...)
  
  // Update fields
  target.setAccountStatus(newStatus);
  target.setStatusReason(reason);
  target.setStatusChangedBy(actorUserId);
  target.setStatusChangedAt(Instant.now());
  
  // Don't start purge; only deleteAccount() does
  if (newStatus == ACTIVE && !target.isDeleted()) {
    target.setScheduledPurgeAt(null);
  }
  
  userRepository.save(target);
  
  // Immediately invalidate all sessions for target user
  invalidateUserSessions(target.getUsername());
  
  // Audit log the change
  auditStatusChange(target, oldStatus, newStatus, reason, actorUserId, ip, ua);
  
  return target;
}
```

**Account Deletion Workflow:**

deleteAccount() method (lines 109-141):
```java
@Transactional
public void deleteAccount(UUID targetUserId, UUID actorUserId, HttpServletRequest httpRequest) {
  // Guard: admin cannot delete own account
  if (targetUserId.equals(actorUserId)) throw new IllegalArgumentException(...)
  
  User target = userRepository.findById(targetUserId).orElseThrow(...)
  
  // Soft-delete: set deleted_at
  target.softDelete();
  
  // Schedule purge 30 days out
  target.setScheduledPurgeAt(Instant.now().plus(30, ChronoUnit.DAYS));
  
  // Set status to DISABLED
  target.setAccountStatus(AccountStatus.DISABLED);
  target.setStatusChangedBy(actorUserId);
  target.setStatusChangedAt(Instant.now());
  
  userRepository.save(target);
  
  // Invalidate sessions
  invalidateUserSessions(target.getUsername());
  
  // Audit log
  auditStatusChange(...)
}
```

**B. Account Status Filter (Per-Request Check)**

### 6. USER MODEL/SCHEMA

**User Entity**
- File: `/home/leul/Documents/w2t63/repo/backend/src/main/java/com/reslife/api/domain/user/User.java`

**Fields:**
```java
UUID id                      // Primary key
String username              // UNIQUE, max 100 chars
String email                 // UNIQUE, max 255 chars
String passwordHash          // BCrypt (cost 12)
String firstName, lastName   // Max 100 chars each
AccountStatus accountStatus  // ENUM: ACTIVE, DISABLED, FROZEN, BLACKLISTED
String statusReason          // Optional reason for status change (TEXT)
UUID statusChangedBy         // UUID of admin who changed it (loose, not FK)
Instant statusChangedAt      // When status last changed
Instant scheduledPurgeAt     // 30-day soft-delete purge timestamp
Instant deletedAt            // Soft-delete timestamp
Instant createdAt, updatedAt // Audit fields
Set<UserRole> userRoles     // ManyToMany with Role table
```

**AccountStatus Enum:**
- File: `/home/leul/Documents/w2t63/repo/backend/src/main/java/com/reslife/api/domain/user/AccountStatus.java`
- Values with semantics:
  - `ACTIVE` — Normal, can log in
  - `DISABLED` — Reversible, scheduled for purge 30 days after explicit deletion
  - `FROZEN` — Temporarily frozen by admin, no purge clock started
  - `BLACKLISTED` — Permanent policy violation block, not reversible via normal flow

**Database Schema (V1__initial_schema.sql, V2__auth_schema.sql):**
```sql
users table:
  - id UUID PRIMARY KEY
  - username VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL
  - email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL
  - password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
  - first_name, last_name VARCHAR(100)
  - account_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE'
  - status_reason TEXT
  - status_changed_by UUID (no FK, survives admin deletion)
  - status_changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
  - scheduled_purge_at TIMESTAMPTZ
  - deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ
  - created_at, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ
  
  Constraints:
  - CHECK (account_status IN ('ACTIVE', 'DISABLED', 'FROZEN', 'BLACKLISTED'))

Indexes:
  - idx_users_account_status (account_status) WHERE != 'ACTIVE'
  - idx_users_scheduled_purge (scheduled_purge_at) WHERE NOT NULL
  - idx_users_deleted_at (deleted_at) WHERE IS NULL

user_roles join table:
  - user_id UUID FK → users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
  - role_id UUID FK → roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
  - assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ
  - PK (user_id, role_id)
```

**updateAccountStatus() method (lines 57-94):**
```java
@Transactional
public User updateAccountStatus(UUID targetUserId, AccountStatus newStatus, String reason,
                                UUID actorUserId, HttpServletRequest httpRequest) {
  // Guard: admin cannot change own status
  if (targetUserId.equals(actorUserId)) throw new IllegalArgumentException(...)
  
  // Load target user
  User target = userRepository.findById(targetUserId).orElseThrow(...)
  
  // Cannot change soft-deleted accounts
  if (target.isDeleted()) throw new IllegalArgumentException(...)
  
  // Update fields
  target.setAccountStatus(newStatus);
  target.setStatusReason(reason);
  target.setStatusChangedBy(actorUserId);
  target.setStatusChangedAt(Instant.now());
  
  // Don't start purge; only deleteAccount() does
  if (newStatus == ACTIVE && !target.isDeleted()) {
    target.setScheduledPurgeAt(null);
  }
  
  userRepository.save(target);
  
  // Immediately invalidate all sessions for target user
  invalidateUserSessions(target.getUsername());
  
  // Audit log the change
  auditStatusChange(target, oldStatus, newStatus, reason, actorUserId, ip, ua);
  
  return target;
}
```

**Account Deletion Workflow:**

deleteAccount() method (lines 109-141):
```java
@Transactional
public void deleteAccount(UUID targetUserId, UUID actorUserId, HttpServletRequest httpRequest) {
  // Guard: admin cannot delete own account
  if (targetUserId.equals(actorUserId)) throw new IllegalArgumentException(...)
  
  User target = userRepository.findById(targetUserId).orElseThrow(...)
  
  // Soft-delete: set deleted_at
  target.softDelete();
  
  // Schedule purge 30 days out
  target.setScheduledPurgeAt(Instant.now().plus(30, ChronoUnit.DAYS));
  
  // Set status to DISABLED
  target.setAccountStatus(AccountStatus.DISABLED);
  target.setStatusChangedBy(actorUserId);
  target.setStatusChangedAt(Instant.now());
  
  userRepository.save(target);
  
  // Invalidate sessions
  invalidateUserSessions(target.getUsername());
  
  // Audit log
  auditStatusChange(...)
}
```

- `purgeExpiredAccounts()` (lines 153-164) — Runs daily at 02:00 UTC, hard-deletes rows past scheduled_purge_at

---

## Assumptions

1. **`@SQLRestriction` bypass via native query** — The `User` entity has `@SQLRestriction("deleted_at IS NULL")` which blocks JPQL from seeing soft-deleted rows. `EntityManager.createNativeQuery()` bypasses this intentionally, so soft-deleted / pending-purge accounts appear when `includeDeleted=true`.

2. **`PATCH` returns `LoginResponse` (no status fields)** — The existing endpoint already returns `LoginResponse` which omits `accountStatus`/`statusReason`. The UI updates those fields optimistically from the values it sent rather than the server response, keeping the contract unchanged.

3. **N+1 on roles** — `UserSummaryDto.from()` triggers lazy-loads for `userRoles` per row. Acceptable for a page of 20 on an infrequent admin screen.

4. **No new Flyway migration needed** — No schema changes; the endpoint queries existing tables.

5. **Page serialization** — Assumes `spring-data-web` auto-configuration is active (it is for Spring Boot; the existing integration-keys page already consumes `d.content`/`d.totalPages`).

---

## What remains manual to verify

- **Build** — Run `cd backend && ./mvnw test` to confirm the new native query compiles and existing tests still pass. Pay attention to `AdminRolePolicyTest` (it mocks `AdminUserService`, so the new method needs no stub there unless the test wires it).
- **Role-change self-guard** — Confirm that if the logged-in admin is in the list, the "Change status" and "Delete" buttons correctly fail at the backend with 400 ("cannot change own account").
- **Soft-delete flow** — Delete a user, tick "Show pending-deletion", verify the row reappears with the purge date and the "Pending purge" label.
- **Search / filter** — Verify search across all four fields and status filter combinations work in the running app.
- **Session invalidation side effect** — Changing a user's status to DISABLED/FROZEN/BLACKLISTED immediately kills their sessions (handled by existing `invalidateUserSessions()` in the service). Worth verifying the admin's own session is not affected.

Clean. Here's the full summary:

---

## Root cause

All four fields (`nextPageUrlPattern`, `itemSelector`, `titleSelector`, `linkSelector`) were read from the JSON config column on every crawl start but never accessed anywhere in `CrawlJobExecution`. `discoverLinks()` queued every href at `currentDepth + 1` with no pagination awareness; `extractAndSaveItems()` always wrote the entire page body as one item via a hardcoded `Map.of(...)`.

---

## Exact behavior added

**`nextPageUrlPattern`** — `discoverLinks()` now evaluates each extracted href against the pattern (via `String.matches()`). A matching link is queued **at the current depth** instead of `currentDepth + 1`, and bypasses both the `followLinks` gate and the `maxDepth` check. Non-matching links keep existing behavior. All that was needed was `java.util.regex` (already imported) and a read of `config.getNextPageUrlPattern()`.

**`itemSelector` / `titleSelector` / `linkSelector`** — When `itemSelector` is non-blank, `extractAndSaveItems()` now calls `tryExtractFromJson()` first:
- If the body is valid JSON **and** `itemSelector` starts with `/` (RFC 6901 JSON Pointer syntax, e.g. `"/results"`): Jackson's `JsonNode.at()` resolves the pointer to an array, then each element is saved as a separate `CrawlItem`. `titleSelector` and `linkSelector` are resolved as JSON Pointers within each element; extracted values appear as top-level `title` and `link` keys in `rawData`, and `CrawlItem.url` is set to the extracted link when available.
- If the body is not valid JSON, or the selector doesn't start with `/` (i.e. looks like a CSS selector): `tryExtractFromJson()` returns `false` and a `DEBUG` log explains **why** (no DOM parser in stack). Execution falls through to the original full-body single-item save.
- No `itemSelector` set: identical to prior behavior.

The item-saving logic was extracted into a `saveItem()` helper (used by both paths), replacing the inlined `Map.of()` with `LinkedHashMap` for stable JSON key order.

---

## Remaining unsupported config fields

**None** — all four fields are now wired. However, **CSS selector / XPath evaluation for HTML responses** remains unimplemented because it requires a DOM parser (e.g. Jsoup) not in the dependency stack. The code documents this with an explanatory comment and falls back cleanly. To enable CSS selectors, add `jsoup` to `pom.xml` and extend `tryExtractFromJson` with an HTML branch.

#### Booking Conversion Computation
Lines 82-112: `computeBookingConversion()` method

**SQL Query 1** (lines 85-86):
```sql
SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM housing_agreements GROUP BY status ORDER BY status
```

**SQL Query 2** (lines 95-102):
```sql
SELECT TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'YYYY-MM') AS month,
       COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM   housing_agreements
WHERE  status = 'SIGNED'
  AND  created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '6 months'
GROUP  BY month ORDER BY month
```

**Tables Queried:** 
- `housing_agreements` — columns: `status`, `created_at`

**Conversion Rate Calculation** (lines 88-92):
- `signed` = count where status = 'SIGNED'
- `expired` = count where status = 'EXPIRED'
- `cancelled` = count where status = 'CANCELLED'
- `decided` = signed + expired + cancelled (excludes PENDING)
- **Rate** = Math.round((signed * 1000.0 / decided)) / 10.0 = percentage rounded to 1 decimal

**JSON Response Fields for booking_conversion** (lines 104-112):


Lines 82-112: `computeBookingConversion()` method

**SQL Query 1** (lines 85-86):
```sql
SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM housing_agreements GROUP BY status ORDER BY status
```

**SQL Query 2** (lines 95-102):
```sql
SELECT TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at), 'YYYY-MM') AS month,
       COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM   housing_agreements
WHERE  status = 'SIGNED'
  AND  created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '6 months'
GROUP  BY month ORDER BY month
```

**Tables Queried:** 
- `housing_agreements` — columns: `status`, `created_at`

**Conversion Rate Calculation** (lines 88-92):
- `signed` = count where status = 'SIGNED'
- `expired` = count where status = 'EXPIRED'
- `cancelled` = count where status = 'CANCELLED'
- `decided` = signed + expired + cancelled (excludes PENDING)
- **Rate** = Math.round((signed * 1000.0 / decided)) / 10.0 = percentage rounded to 1 decimal

## Summary

**Option chosen: Option 1 — remove unsupported syntax from the template.**

**Why it is lower risk:**  
Option 2 (adding `{{#key}}...{{/key}}` section support) would require a regex with `DOTALL` mode on the renderer, creating a new code path that every template passes through on every notification send. A bug there silently corrupts all notifications. Option 1 is a single SQL `UPDATE` in an isolated migration — worst case it only affects `onboarding.welcome`, it's auditable, and it's reversible.

There was also no code to write: the renderer already handles the revised template correctly since `{{tasks}}` is a plain placeholder that maps to whatever pre-formatted string the caller provides (empty string when no tasks).

**Files changed:**
- `frontend/src/pages/ResidentFormPage.jsx` — `EnrollmentSection` and `MoveInSection` building fields now use `<BuildingSelector>` instead of the raw `<select>` with `__other__`

**Edge cases handled by `BuildingSelector`:**
- Selecting "Other / unlisted" clears the value and shows a text input — `__other__` is never stored in parent state
- Typing in the text input writes the raw string directly via `onChange(v)`
- If the form is loaded in edit mode with a persisted building name not in the current list (e.g. renamed building), `isOther` initialises to `true` so the name appears in the text input rather than silently disappearing
- When no buildings are returned from the API (`buildings.length === 0`), both sections fall back to the plain `<input>` unchanged
- `FormField`'s injected `style` and `aria-invalid` props are forwarded to both the `<select>` and the fallback `<input>` inside `BuildingSelector`
